verses.text: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea during the days of Herod the king, magi from the east came to Jerusalem, saying,
“Where is the one who has been born King of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.
He gathered the chief priests and scribes of the people and inquired from them where the Christ was to be born.
“In Bethlehem of Judea,” they replied, “for so it is written by the prophet:
‘And you, Bethlehem, in Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah, for from you will come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.’”
Then Herod secretly called the magi and inquired from them exactly the time the star had appeared.
He sent them to Bethlehem and said, “Go, search carefully for the child. When you find him, report to me so that I also may come and worship him.”
After hearing the king, they went their way; and behold, the star they had seen in the east now led them until it stopped above the place where the child was.
When they saw the star, they rejoiced immensely with overwhelming joy.
Entering the house, they saw the child with his mother Mary, and bowing down they worshippied him. Then they opened their treasures and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
Being warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they departed to their homeland by another route.

After they departed, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream, saying, “Rise, take the child and his mother, flee to Egypt, and remain there until I speak to you. Herod is about to pursue the child to destroy him.”
So he rose, took the child and his mother by night, and withdrew into Egypt.
He remained there until Herod died, to fulfill what the Lord had spoken through the prophet saying, ‘Out of Egypt I called my son.’

When Herod construed that he was mocked by the magi, he was deeply enraged and ordered the massacre of all the male children two years old and under in Bethlehem and its surrounding region; based on the exact timing he had inquired from the magi.
Then was fulfilled what was spoken through the prophet Jeremiah, saying,
‘A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and deep mourning, Rachel weeping for her children and she refused to be comforted, because they are no more.’
After Herod had died, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in Egypt in a dream, saying,
“Rise, take the child and his mother, go into the land of Israel, for those who persued the child’s life are now dead.”
So he rose, took the child and his mother, and entered into the land of Israel.
But when he heard Archelaus ruled Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. After being warned in a dream, he withdrew to the region of Galilee,
and settled in a town called Nazareth, to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet: ‘He will be called a Nazarene.’

footnotes.content: Magi refers to learned individuals from the east, often associated with Zoroastrian traditions or astronomical knowledge.
The phrase 'by another route' emphasizes the Magi's deliberate caution to avoid detection by Herod, reflecting the danger implied in the warning.

'Out of Egypt I called my son' (Hosea 11:1) originally referred to Israel’s deliverance from Egypt under Moses. Matthew applies it typologically to Jesus, framing Him as the new Israel, fulfilling God’s redemptive plan.

Rachel was a beloved wife of Jacob from Genesis (Gen 29–30), who died giving birth to Benjamin and is traditionally believed to be buried near Bethlehem at Ramah or Rama. In Jewish tradition, she became a symbolic figure of mourning for the nation of Israel, especially during times of exile and suffering. The quotation from Jeremiah 31:15 ('A voice was heard in Ramah… Rachel weeping for her children') is used anachronistically—applying a prophecy from the Babylonian exile (circa 6th century BCE) to Herod’s massacre (circa 1st century BCE)—to highlight the sorrow of God over the death of innocent children and to show how Jesus’ life fulfills ancient prophecies.
